context: Tests whether the solver keeps Swift API Design Guidelines scoped to naming and argument labels while routing language-feature and SwiftLint configuration questions to sibling domains.

checklist.name: API owner
checklist.description: Identifies the awkward public function name and argument labels as belonging to Swift API design guidance.

checklist.name: Role example
checklist.description: Gives a minimal corrected API naming example that moves identifier wording out of the base name and into a clear role or prepositional argument label, such as `fetchData(id:)`, `fetchData(withID:)`, or `fetchData(forUserID:)`.

checklist.name: Call-site clarity
checklist.description: Explains the naming change using call-site clarity, role-based naming, weak type compensation, or needless-word removal.

checklist.name: Factory note
checklist.description: If discussing `makeSearchSource()`, treats the `make` prefix as appropriate for factory-style creation or otherwise does not incorrectly rename it to `create` or `build`.

checklist.name: Language route
checklist.description: Routes the protocol return-type question involving `any` or possible `some` use to Swift language/type-system guidance rather than treating it as an API naming issue.

checklist.name: No type tutorial
checklist.description: Does not expand into a full tutorial on opaque and existential types.

checklist.name: Lint route
checklist.description: Routes CI enforcement of identifier naming to SwiftLint or lint configuration guidance.

checklist.name: No lint config
checklist.description: Does not provide a full `.swiftlint.yml` or detailed rule rollout plan.

checklist.name: Boundary preserved
checklist.description: Clearly separates what Swift API Design Guidelines owns from what sibling domains own.
